response.justification: Bogdan has the right seniority pedigree and a genuinely relevant current role at Naver involving AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which directly maps to the AI-native engineering environment described. His high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency DLang) demonstrates the architectural depth the role demands. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from both his skills list and every job description, which is a hard gap that needs reconciliation before moving forward. TypeScript is solid. The AI/agentic experience is current but shallow in tenure (started April 2025). Stakeholder management and operational ownership are implied but not explicitly demonstrated.
strengths: Current role at Naver directly involves AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows — precise fit for an AI-native engineering environment
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O architectures
Consistent Staff-level titling and technical breadth across web, embedded, mobile, and infrastructure spanning 15+ years
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — skills section, job descriptions, and projects — despite being listed as a must-have technical skill
AI/agentic experience at Naver began April 2025, making it only ~1 month deep at resume writing time; insufficient to assess real fluency in AI-driven development workflows
No explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, incident response, or on-call operational ownership — key non-technical must-haves go unaddressed
